stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the established treatment) yields a guaranteed return of $95K. Option B (the experimental treatment) depends on patient response rate: with probability 32-55% (uncertain), it yields $154K; otherwise it yields $21K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in patient response rate affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $95K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $63.6K to $94.2K. Point estimate: $79.5K. Option A is preferred at most assumptions (EV_A > EV_B when P(good) < 55.6%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 55.6%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
